records.interpretation_note: Compare against the 'same activity, same risks, same regulation' principle HKMA applies; note interaction with SFC tokenisation/STO perimeter where settlement touches tokenised funds.

records.requirement_summary: CLARITY Act Sec. 404(c)(1) prohibits a covered party from paying any form of interest or yield to a restricted recipient (A) solely in connection with the holding of payment stablecoins, or (B) on a payment stablecoin balance in a manner economically or functionally equivalent to interest on an interest-bearing bank deposit. Bona fide activity- or transaction-based rewards are permitted, with scope to be defined by joint SEC/CFTC/Treasury rulemaking. <VERIFY exact carve-out wording against the engrossed bill text>

requirement_structured.activity_based_rewards: permitted, scope TBD by joint rulemaking

source.primary: Digital Asset Market CLARITY Act of 2025 (H.R. 3633), Sec. 404(c)(1); Senate Banking Committee text (May 12, 2026)
source.url: <VERIFY canonical link: congress.gov H.R.3633 / Senate Banking published text>

secondary.citation: Not yet law as of June 2026; placed on Senate Legislative Calendar (General Orders) June 1, 2026; awaits reconciliation with Senate Agriculture text and House H.R. 3633, plus 60-vote floor passage

records.interpretation_note: ANCHOR / LIVE WINDOW. The bona-fide-activity boundary is unsettled and still being shaped: SEC Crypto Task Force written-input channel is open now; the main comment window is the post-enactment joint NPRM. Routing into 1940-Act registered tokenised MMFs is argued to fall outside the prohibition because the operative step is the user's signed authorisation, not the holding. Track NPRM timing for a substantive comment.

corridors.name: HK to Brazil — USD stablecoin B2B settlement
corridors.flow: USD-referenced stablecoin used for B2B cross-border settlement, originating HK side, terminating Brazil side

boundary_analysis.gate: <VERIFY: HK perimeter — FRS regime (Cap. 656) where issuance/distribution touches; SFC tokenisation/STO perimeter where settlement touches tokenised funds>

Brazil FX perimeter. Two diverging rails: (i) eFX rail — BCB Resolution 561 prohibits eFX providers from using stablecoins/crypto to settle with overseas counterparties (effective Oct 1, 2026); (ii) VASP rail — BCB Resolution 521 permits authorised VASPs to use stablecoins for international payments inside the FX market.
boundary_analysis.clears: Authorised-VASP-in-FX channel (Res 521), subject to per-transaction limits (USD 100k pure VASP / USD 500k FX-authorised FI), monthly FX reporting (Annex II-A), custody segregation (Res 520: MPC/HSM, proof-of-reserves, biennial audit).
boundary_analysis.breaks: eFX back-end stablecoin settlement rail (Res 561) — closing. Algorithmic / non-auditable-reserve stablecoins prohibited.

corridors.key_constraint: A single USD-stablecoin instrument cannot serve both HK and Brazil as one uniform rail: the Brazil side forces the flow through the authorised-VASP-in-FX channel, not the closing eFX rail. This is the binding 'regulatory geography' constraint the corridor must be architected around.
corridors.us_doctrinal_link: Same underlying question as US Sec. 404 bona-fide-activity carve-out: what activity is permitted on the regulated stablecoin rail. US = the anchor instance; see data/us-pss-permitted_activity_yield-001.yaml.
sources: BCB Resolution 521/2025; BCB Resolution 561 (Apr 30, 2026); BCB Resolution 520/2025 (custody)
